title: Load Balancing Over Time
breadcrumbs: Deployment Considerations for Performance and Scalability
text: Load Balancing Over Time A system can be overloaded during certain periods and be almost idle during other periods. If this is the case, there are opportunities for more efficient use of the system. You can: Suspend jobs with low priority to times with low system activity Combine jobs with low priority into a single batch job and submitting this job as a low-priority background process The data for system load over time, such as the number of concurrent users and the access stress rates, are collected during inception and analyzed using techniques such as access path analysis. The following illustration shows a possible result: Figure 1. Sample Load Balance Data The Uniface Router can balance loads over time. During peak times, there can be a pool of Application Servers handling incoming client requests. As requests come in, the Uniface Router gives each request to an available, matching server that has handled most requests. After the peak, as client requests become fewer, many servers become idle. You can ensure that the Uniface Router stops these idle servers by setting the /maxidle setting in the UST (server type) definition. Related Links /maxidle Specify the maximum time a shared Uniface Server (identified by its server name (UST)), is allowed to be idle (not handling any requests). Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
